creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of code produced with AI.
contentSnippet: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of code produced with AI.

creator: Tim Fernholz
title: (No AI) OpenAI acquires Promptfoo to secure its AI agents

content: This deal underscores how frontier labs are scrambling to prove their technology can be used safely in critical business operations.

title: (No AI) Anthropic is suing the Department of Defense
author: Hayden Field
content:
![Warplanes being targeted with AI overlays.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STKS515_CYBER_WARFARE2_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Anthropic has sued the US government over its [designation as a supply-chain risk](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890347/pentagon-anthropic-supply-chain-risk), the latest move in a weekslong [battle between it and the Pentagon](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/886082/ai-vs-the-pentagon-killer-robots-mass-surveillance-and-red-lines) over the acceptable use cases for its military AI tech. The suit, filed in a California district court, accuses the Trump administration of illegally punishing the company for setting "red lines" on mass domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons.

"The federal government retaliated against a leading frontier AI developer for adhering to its protected viewpoint on a subject of great public significance - AI safety and the limitations of its own AI models - in violation of the Constitution …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/891377/anthropic-dod-lawsuit)
contentSnippet:
Anthropic has sued the US government over its designation as a supply-chain risk, the latest move in a weekslong battle between it and the Pentagon over the acceptable use cases for its military AI tech. The suit, filed in a California district court, accuses the Trump administration of illegally punishing the company for setting "red lines" on mass domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons.
"The federal government retaliated against a leading frontier AI developer for adhering to its protected viewpoint on a subject of great public significance - AI safety and the limitations of its own AI models - in violation of the Constitution …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The suit, filed in a California district court, accuses the Trump administration of illegally punishing the company for setting "red […]

title: (No AI) X says you can block Grok from editing your photos
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![The option to block Grok from editing images, as seen under the photo upload settings on X.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Grok-block-edit-options-hed.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Pay attention to that small print about tagging @Grok, this new toggle has disappointing limitations. | Image by The Verge / xAI

X has introduced a new feature that makes it slightly harder for other users to manipulate your uploaded images with the Grok chatbot. As reported by [_Social Media Today_](https://www.socialmediatoday.com/news/x-formerly-twitter-adds-option-to-restrict-grok-image-variations/814140/) and verified by _The Verge_, a new toggle within the image upload settings on the X iOS app says it can "block modifications by Grok" when enabled. But it doesn't _actually_ [prevent Grok from editing your photos](https://www.theverge.com/news/859715/x-grok-ai-deepfakes).

The small print underneath the feature's name reveals a significant limitation: users can only "prevent @Grok from modifying this content." In our testing, the toggle only blocks the mechanism of tagging the xAI chatbot in replies to an image on X, alongside editing ins …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/891352/x-grok-xai-edit-blocker-photo-toggle)
contentSnippet:

But it doesn't actually prevent Grok from editing your photos.
The small print underneath the feature's name reveals a significant limitation: users can only "prevent @Grok from modifying this content." In our testing, the toggle only blocks the mechanism of tagging the xAI chatbot in replies to an image on X, alongside editing ins …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) OpenAI to acquire Promptfoo
content: OpenAI is acquiring Promptfoo, an AI security platform that helps enterprises identify and remediate vulnerabilities in AI systems during development.
contentSnippet: OpenAI is acquiring Promptfoo, an AI security platform that helps enterprises identify and remediate vulnerabilities in AI systems during development.

creator: Jason Parham
title: (No AI) Feeld Was a Dating App for the Freaks. Now Some People Call It ‘Normie Hell’
dc:creator: Jason Parham
content: The app that catered to unconventional kinks is gaining steam among daters with vanilla preferences—and some aren’t happy about it.
contentSnippet: The app that catered to unconventional kinks is gaining steam among daters with vanilla preferences—and some aren’t happy about it.
